repaired_question_bank.question: When is a generative model especially preferable to a deterministic one-to-one mapping?
choices: When the same condition y can correspond to many different plausible outputs x.
When the output always has exactly one correct answer.
When only a single discrete label needs to be predicted.
When no new samples need to be generated.
repaired_question_bank.explanation: If one condition can lead to many valid outcomes, the model should represent the conditional distribution rather than returning only one answer. That is why generative models are especially useful when outputs are inherently ambiguous.

repaired_question_bank.question: What is the main difference between an explicit-density model and an implicit-density model?
choices: An explicit model lets you read off p(x), while an implicit model mainly lets you sample without directly exposing p(x).
An explicit model cannot generate samples, while an implicit model cannot be trained.
An explicit model is only for images, while an implicit model is only for text.
An implicit model is always more accurate than an explicit model in every setting.
repaired_question_bank.explanation: Explicit-density models directly provide the density or probability assigned to a sample. Implicit models may learn to generate samples from a distribution without necessarily making p(x) directly accessible.

transcript_quotes: sample without directly reading out p(x)

repaired_question_bank.question: Which model pairing is correct under the taxonomy discussed in the lecture?
choices: GANs are direct implicit models, while diffusion models are iterative implicit models.
GANs are explicit exact-density models, while diffusion models are discriminative.
VAEs are direct implicit models, while autoregressive models are iterative implicit models.
Autoregressive models are implicit, while GANs are explicit approximate-density models.
repaired_question_bank.explanation: In the taxonomy presented in the lecture, GANs are treated as implicit models that generate samples directly, while diffusion models generate samples through an iterative process and therefore belong to the iterative implicit branch.
